mnemonics.title: MTBF and MTTR Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: MTBF = Mean Time Between Failures (Uptime average), MTTR = Mean Time To Repair (Fix time). Remember: 'B' for Between (between failures), 'R' for Repair.

mnemonics.title: Capacity Planning Types
mnemonics.content: Short: Tactical (0-3 months), Medium: Strategic (3-12 months), Long: Visionary (1-3 years). Think 'TSV' = Time, Strategic, Vision.

mnemonics.title: Scaling Types
mnemonics.content: Vertical = Up (bigger hardware), Horizontal = Out (more servers). Remember: UPgrade = Vertical, HOrizontal = OUT (H-O).

acronyms.usage: Short-term memory storage for active processes

visualTips.visualization: Think of a pie chart: MTBF is the 'up' slice, MTTR is the 'down' slice. Availability = Up slice / Total pie. As MTTR gets smaller, the 'up' slice grows.
Vertical scaling = stacking blocks UP (taller tower). Horizontal scaling = spreading blocks OUT (wider arrangement).
Graph showing usage over time - an upward slope indicates need for more capacity. Draw a 'threshold line' at 80% - when graph approaches it, plan expansion.

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant owner predicting busy seasons - hiring staff and stocking ingredients before customers arrive, not after they're waiting.
MTBF is like how long a car runs without breaking down; MTTR is how long repairs take. Good car = high MTBF, low MTTR.
Like your normal body temperature (98.6°F) - deviations indicate something's wrong. System baselines show when performance is abnormal.
Like a fuel gauge warning light at 1/4 tank - gives early warning to refuel before running empty.
Vertical = adding more floors to a building (taller). Horizontal = building more buildings in a campus (wider).
